mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising core inflation erodes real yields and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income directly supports consumer expenditure and corporate earnings growth, generating positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5% signals a supply-side shock or demand collapse that disrupts corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops > 1.5%, or the signal produces false positives in > 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and real disposable income indicates a self-reinforcing consumption cycle that expands corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, rising housing starts amplify the positive earnings momentum by signaling strong credit conditions and construction sector activity, boosting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher when both GDPC1 and HOUST rise, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods.
